source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/02_ADD (HAD1417MCW)/▶ FDA/참조 문서/1. Risk Management/1. Risk Management Plan (Doc. No. RMP-01, Rev.0).docx

sourceSummary.oneLine: Risk Management Plan RMP-01 Rev.0 defines a product life-cycle risk management process using EN ISO 14971:2012 and related medical device standards.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Used to plan, assign responsibilities for, review, and document risk management activities from design and development through production and post-production monitoring.
keyPoints: Risk management activities follow EN ISO 14971:2012 and are performed throughout manufacture, testing, installation, management, development, production, and post-production.
The plan defines severity and probability scales, calculates risk level as Severity x Probability, and classifies risk levels 1 to 4 as acceptable and 5 to 25 as unacceptable.

Post-production information is collected from internal sources such as PMCF, CAPA reports, customer complaints, service records, and surveys, and external sources such as recalls, incident reports, literature, similar devices, laws, standards, and clinical trial information.

entities.evidence: Note 1. Recall report about similar products of FDA (2009 ~ 2018)

entities.name: Medical Device Regulation (EU) 2017/745
entities.evidence: Medical Device Regulation (EU) 2017/745 of the European Parliament and of the council.

entities.evidence: Quality manager shall assess the suitability of the following information which is collected and analyzed variety information about post-production

entities.name: Representative Director
entities.evidence: Representative Director shall ensure that the necessary resources are available when needed in risk management.

concepts.name: Risk management process
concepts.evidence: Our risk management activities follow EN ISO 14971:2012

concepts.name: Risk management plan scope
concepts.evidence: The Risk management plan continued for plan, development, design and production, post-production information management of 모델명 during life cycle.

concepts.name: Post-production information
concepts.evidence: Post-production Information should be collected through the collection methods such as surveys, customer satisfaction surveys, customer complaints (Internet, telephone, etc.), or articles or news reports.

concepts.name: Residual risk
concepts.evidence: risk remaining after risk control measures have been taken

concepts.name: Risk management file
concepts.evidence: set of records and other documents that are produced by risk management

evidence.evidenceText: Revision History No. Rev. No. Description of changes Date 1 0 Established by manufacturer’s Risk Management Policy 2018.12.10

evidence.claim: The risk management plan follows EN ISO 14971:2012.
evidence.evidenceText: Our risk management activities follow EN ISO 14971:2012

evidence.claim: The purpose of the risk management plan is to establish a risk management process according to EN ISO 14971:2012 and apply EN 60601-1 3.1 Ed.
evidence.evidenceText: The purpose of risk management plan is applied EN 60601-1 3.1 Ed. according to EN ISO 14971:2012 by establish risk management process.

evidence.claim: The plan applies risk management over the entire product life cycle.
evidence.evidenceText: We shall apply to entire life cycle of the following product.

evidence.claim: The risk management policy requires activities throughout manufacture, testing, installation, management, development, and production.
evidence.evidenceText: Therefore, we will perform the risk management activities throughout the entire process Manufacture, testing, installation and management, development of the production.

evidence.claim: Risk acceptability criteria are based on applicable national or regional regulations, relevant international standards, state of the art, and stakeholder concerns.
evidence.evidenceText: this policy shall ensure that criteria are based upon applicable national or regional regulations and relevant International Standards, and take into account available information such as the generally accepted state of the art and known stakeholder concerns

evidence.claim: Risk level is calculated as severity multiplied by probability.
Risk levels 1 to 4 are defined as acceptable, and risk levels 5 to 25 are defined as unacceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Green Zone Acceptable Region Risk Level: 1~4 Red Zone Unacceptable Region Risk Level: 5~25

evidence.claim: All risks are reduced as far as possible without economic considerations.
evidence.evidenceText: All risks reduced “as far as possible” without economic considerations.

evidence.claim: Post-production information should be collected from internal and external information sources.
evidence.evidenceText: The get information method of Post production information should be collected according to the internal information and the external information.

evidence.claim: Internal post-production information sources include PMCF, CAPA reports, customer complaints, service information, and customer satisfaction surveys.
evidence.evidenceText: Internal (1) PMCF (Post Market Clinical Follow up) (2) Corrective and preventive actions related reports (3) Customer Complaints (4) A / S recorded and maintained the information obtained during maintenance (5) Customer satisfaction surveys

evidence.claim: External post-production information sources include medical incident reports, academic papers, expert opinions, similar device information, laws and standards trends, and clinical trial information.
evidence.evidenceText: External (1) Reporting medical incidents (Recall, the side effects reported cases of domestic and foreign) (2) Related academic papers and expert opinions (3) Information related similar medical device (4) Establishment and revised trends in international and domestic laws / standards (5) In the process of performing clinical trials information

evidence.claim: The Quality Manager assesses the suitability of collected and analyzed post-production information.
evidence.evidenceText: Quality manager shall assess the suitability of the following information which is collected and analyzed variety information about post-production

evidence.claim: Additional risk control measures may require changes to the risk management plan and risk management activities.
evidence.evidenceText: If it is determined that additional risk control measures are necessary in order to present the suitability evaluation factors should be identified or modified to perform additional changes to the risk management plan and risk management activities (risk analysis, risk assessment and risk control)

reviewFlags.reason: Product name and model name are placeholders in the source text, shown as 제품명 and 모델명; no specific product model is present in the document body.
Document date is ambiguous: preparation, review, and approval dates are 2018.02.10, while revision history for Rev.0 lists 2018.12.10.
Authority or jurisdiction is ambiguous: source metadata/path indicates FDA, while the document body also references EU MDR, MEDDEV documents, and EN/IEC standards.
Several personnel names are placeholders, shown as 담당자 성명.
